steps.title: Read set A from the dot plot
steps.content: The dot plot for set A shows capacities centered with a mean of $8\,\mu$F and a spread from the smallest to the largest value of $12\,\mu$F (the range).

steps.title: Apply the shift to get set B
steps.content: Every capacitor in set B equals the matching set A capacitor plus $17\,\mu$F. Adding a constant to every value shifts the mean by that same constant but leaves the range unchanged.

steps.title: Compute set B's mean and range
steps.content: Mean of B $= 8 + 17 = 25\,\mu$F. Range of B $= 12\,\mu$F (unchanged).

steps.title: Match against the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A (mean $8$, range $12$): wrong mean — the shift of $+17$ was ignored.
  • Choice B (mean $8$, range $29$): wrong mean and a range cannot grow from a constant shift.
  • Choice C (mean $25$, range $12$): matches both values.
  • Choice D (mean $25$, range $29$): right mean but range incorrectly enlarged by $17$.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
